HPI: Child was brought by her mother with a 3-day history of high fever (up to 40.2C at home), rigors, vomiting, and progressive lethargy. She became increasingly lethargic and was referred to district hospital, then transferred here. Mother reports the child has had 2 prior episodes of uncomplicated malaria in the past year. The child sleeps under an insecticide-treated net intermittently. She is not on malaria chemoprophylaxis. She has received all EPI vaccinations per Tanzania schedule. No known drug allergies.

Physical Exam: Lethargic but rousable child. Severely pale conjunctivae and palms. Jaundiced sclerae. No neck stiffness. Tachycardic, no murmurs. Tachypneic with deep (Kussmaul) breathing. Abdomen: hepatomegaly 3 cm below costal margin, splenomegaly 4 cm. No skin petechiae or purpura.

Blinding test score (BCS): 3/5 (obtunded but localizes pain)

Labs: Thick blood smear positive for P. falciparum, parasite density 120,000/uL (hyperparasitaemia). Haemoglobin 5.2 g/dL (severe malarial anaemia). WBC 14.8, Plt 68 (thrombocytopenia). 5. Tepid sponging and paracetamol 15 mg/kg for fever.
6. Nil by mouth until improved consciousness. IV maintenance fluids (4:1 dextrose-saline).
7. Monitor for complications: cerebral malaria, renal failure, DIC, blackwater fever.
8. Repeat parasite density at 12 and 24 hours to confirm clearance. CBC and chemistry daily.
9. Counsel mother on consistent ITN use and prompt care-seeking for fever.
